sections.content: Purple bar click did nothing. I've wasted too many actions. Let me reset my understanding and try a systematic approach. Current: left green at 9-11 (center W), right green at 15-17 (center c). Let me try to position both greens at their targets (left c-square and bridge) with centers matching, then click center.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current: left green at 9-11 (center W, selected), right green at 15-17 (center c)
# Target: left green at 12-14 (c-square), right green at 30-32 (bridge)
# Need centers: left c, right W (or left W, right c)

# Step 1: RIGHT to move left green to 12-14
result = action(['RIGHT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"  Green: boundary={n['boundary']}")
        for c in n['children']:
            cn = [x for x in seg['nodes'] if x['id'] == c][0]
            print(f"    Center: color={cn['color']}")

# Step 2: Click right green to select it (and swap centers)
result2 = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 31, 'col': 16}])
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nAfter clicking right green:")
for n in seg2['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"  Green: boundary={n['boundary']}")
        for c in n['children']:
            cn = [x for x in seg2['nodes'] if x['id'] == c][0]
            print(f"    Center: color={cn['color']}")

# Step 3: RIGHT x5 to move right green to 30-32
result3 = action(['RIGHT'] * 5)
seg3 = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nAfter 5 RIGHTs:")
for n in seg3['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"  Green: boundary={n['boundary']}")
        for c in n['children']:
            cn = [x for x in seg3['nodes'] if x['id'] == c][0]
            print(f"    Center: color={cn['color']}")

# Step 4: Click center
result4 = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 31, 'col': 32}])
print(f"\nAfter clicking center: board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
